implementation_notes: Implementation via task-work delegation. Files planned: 0, Files actual: 0
requirements_addressed: AC-008 JSON array of WebResult dicts

completion_promises.criterion_text: `src/jarvis/tools/general.py` exposes `search_web(query: str, max_results: int = 5) -> str` decorated with `@tool(parse_docstring=True)`.
completion_promises.evidence: search_web is defined in src/jarvis/tools/general.py with the exact signature `search_web(query: str, max_results: int = 5) -> str` and is decorated with `@tool(parse_docstring=True)`. It is exported in `__all__`. Tests in TestAC001SearchWebExposed verify it is a BaseTool, the tool name matches, and the args schema exposes `query` and `max_results`.

The search_web docstring text is reproduced verbatim from docs/design/FEAT-JARVIS-002/contracts/API-tools.md §1.2, including Unicode en-dashes (`1–3s`, `1–10`) and em-dashes. TestAC002DocstringMatchesContract.test_underlying_function_docstring_matches asserts `inspect.getdoc(search_web.func) == EXPECTED_DOCSTRING` against the byte-for-byte expected text.

completion_promises.criterion_text: Uses the `langchain-tavily` provider wrapper; returns `ERROR: config_missing — tavily_api_key not set in JarvisConfig` when `config.tavily_api_key is None`.
completion_promises.evidence: TavilyProvider.search() calls `langchain_tavily.TavilySearch` (lazy import). `_resolve_api_key()` returns None for None / empty SecretStr, and search_web returns `ERROR: config_missing — tavily_api_key not set in JarvisConfig` exactly. TestAC003ConfigMissing covers None config, None tavily_api_key, and empty SecretStr.

completion_promises.criterion_text: Rejects empty query with `ERROR: invalid_query — query must be non-empty`.
completion_promises.evidence: search_web returns `ERROR: invalid_query — query must be non-empty` for empty, whitespace-only, and tab/newline queries. TestAC004InvalidQuery parametrises over '', '   ', '\t\n'. Validated even when no JarvisConfig is wired, proving the check fires before config inspection.

completion_promises.criterion_text: Rejects `max_results` outside `[1, 10]` with `ERROR: invalid_max_results — must be between 1 and 10, got <n>` (boundaries: 1 and 10 accept; 0 and 11 reject).
completion_promises.evidence: search_web returns `ERROR: invalid_max_results — must be between 1 and 10, got <n>` for 0, -1, 11, 100; accepts 1, 5, 10 inclusively. TestAC005InvalidMaxResults parametrises both directions and asserts the provider is called with the propagated max_results for the boundary values.

completion_promises.criterion_text: On provider non-success response returns `DEGRADED: provider_unavailable — Tavily returned <status>` per ASSUM-005 exact format.
completion_promises.evidence: Provider exceptions (ValueError, TypeError, RuntimeError, plus any other Exception subclass) and dict responses with an 'error' key, missing 'results', non-list 'results', or unexpected 'status' all return `DEGRADED: provider_unavailable — Tavily returned <status>`. TestAC006DegradedProviderUnavailable covers raising provider, error-field provider, and missing-results provider.

completion_promises.criterion_text: Returns hostile snippet content verbatim in `WebResult.snippet` — no sanitisation (ASSUM-004). No side-effecting tool calls from inside `search_web`.
completion_promises.evidence: _coerce_results passes `entry.get('content')` straight into WebResult.snippet without escaping. TestAC007HostileSnippetPassthrough injects a snippet containing prompt-injection text, <script>, template syntax, SQL, null escapes, and RTL-override characters and asserts the parsed JSON entry's snippet matches byte-for-byte. search_web makes no tool calls beyond delegating to TavilyProvider.search.

completion_promises.criterion_text: Returns JSON array of `WebResult` dicts on success.
completion_promises.evidence: On the success path search_web returns `json.dumps(_coerce_results(...))`. TestAC008ReturnsJsonWebResultArray parses the result, asserts it is a list, that each entry has exactly the keys {title, url, snippet, score}, and that each entry round-trips through `WebResult(**entry)`. The truncation test confirms the response is capped at max_results entries.

Two layers of try/except in search_web: an inner block converts provider exceptions into DEGRADED, and an outer block catches any other failure mode (including impossible cases). The _coerce_results helper itself swallows row-level validation errors. TestAC009NeverRaises asserts that TypeError-raising and garbage-returning providers both produce a DEGRADED string rather than propagating.

completion_promises.criterion_text: Seam test: with `fake_tavily_response` fixture, calling via `assemble_tool_list`-wired supervisor returns parseable JSON matching the WebResult shape.
completion_promises.evidence: The `fake_tavily_response` fixture monkeypatches `general._provider_factory` to a stub provider that records calls and returns canned data. TestAC010SeamTestThroughToolWrapper invokes search_web via the BaseTool.invoke() protocol (the same surface assemble_tool_list will register on the supervisor) and verifies the result is a JSON string parseable into a list of WebResult-shaped dicts. assemble_tool_list itself is TASK-J002-015 and is not in scope for this task; the seam test exercises the tool-protocol boundary directly.

completion_promises.criterion_text: All modified files pass project-configured lint/format checks with zero errors.
completion_promises.evidence: `ruff check src/jarvis/tools/general.py tests/test_search_web.py` returns `All checks passed!` and `ruff format --check` reports no diff after running `ruff format`. Per-file `# ruff: noqa: RUF002` (source) and `# ruff: noqa: RUF001` (test) directives are added with explanatory comments because the contract docstring requires Unicode en-dashes byte-for-byte.
